title: Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Nagzira wildlife sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Bhandara and Gondia districts of Maharashtra state in India.
overview.description: Nagzira wildlife sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in the Bhandara and Gondia districts of Maharashtra state in India. It harbours many fish species, 34 species of mammals, 166 species of birds, 36 species of reptiles and four amphibian species. The sanctuary is known for its rich biodiversity, supporting a variety of aquatic and terrestrial species This national park’s invertebrate fauna includes a number of butterflies and other insect species. Large wild mammals present include the Bengal tiger, Indian leopard, gaur, sambar, nilgai, chital, wild boar, sloth bear, Indian muntjac, Indian spot

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur (99 km) and the nearest railway station is Nagpur Junction (93 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nagzira Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chorkhamara Dam.
